data.title: US Senator Lummis lauds Commerce Dept. for posting GDP data on blockchain platforms, calling it a "historic move." Coinbase CEO Armstrong praises decision, credits Commerce Secretary Lutnick.
data.description: Supporters of cryptocurrency celebrated the decision by the U.S. government to post Gross Domestic Product (GDP) figures on multiple blockchain platforms. Senator Cynthia Lummis from Wyoming described the move by the Commerce Department as "historic," highlighting that it will solidify America's position as a leader in digital innovation and transparency. She expressed gratitude to President of the United States (@POTUS) and Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick for their guidance. Similarly, Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong lauded the decision, emphasizing the significance of integrating government data onto blockchain technology, attributing this development to Lutnick's leadership. This move is seen as a crucial step in utilizing blockchain for smart contracts and oracles. Armstrong expressed Coinbase's pride in supporting the Commerce Department in this endeavor. For further details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.
Thailand’s FinMin projects under 50% local content for goods exported to the U.S.

data.title: Court rules against DOL withholding contractor demographic data, impacting transparency efforts and records spanning three presidencies.
data.description: Last week, a federal appeals court dealt a setback to the Labor Department by ruling against their efforts to keep workforce demographic data of federal government contractors confidential. This decision comes after a prolonged struggle spanning three presidential terms.

data.title: Pro-trade groups are strategizing against Trump's tariffs, preparing for more outreach to secure relief.
data.description: Advocates for international trade are not persuaded by President Donald Trump's fondness for tariffs, and are working on increasing their efforts to seek assistance.

data.title: Trump administration undoes Biden-era policies disliked by big banks; includes restrictions on late fees, overdraft charges.
data.description: For several months, the Trump administration has been focused on reversing policies from the Biden era that big banks strongly dislike. These policies include limitations on credit card late fees and overdraft charges.

data.title: American Rebel Holdings clears up confusion regarding an amended S-1 filing on Aug 28; confirms no new filing, just a June 3 amend and June 27 declaration.
data.description: Following the circulation of misinformation in August 28 Benzinga posts, the Company received numerous investor inquiries. It clarified that there has been no recent filing of a new S-1/A. The mentioned amendment was actually filed on June 3, 2025, with the referenced registration becoming effective on June 27, 2025.

data.title: Lido DAO price battles at $1.24 amid -2.84% daily drop. Technicals show mixed signals: RSI at 48.19, nearing Bollinger Band support in strong bullish trend.
data.description: Lido DAO is currently trading at $1.24, experiencing a daily decline of -2.84%. The Relative Strength Index (RSI) for LDO stands at 48.19, indicating a neutral position. The price is approaching the Bollinger Band support level, although it is part of a very strong bullish trend overall.

data.title: In July, job cuts increased by 140% from a year ago, while Fed's inflation gauge surged, boosting S&P 500.
data.description: Recent data indicates a significant increase in job cuts, with a staggering jump of 140% compared to the previous year in July. Additionally, the Federal Reserve's preferred measure of inflation showed a notable rise. This surge in layoff numbers coincided with a rise in the S&P 500 index.

data.title: Soma Gold's Q2 2025 revenue was $23.0M, net income $1.6M, EBITDA $9.1M, sold 5,200 AuEq ounces. Operations review: 5,377 AuEq ounces produced in Q2, $5.7M income from mining operations. (Character count: 213)
data.description: Soma Gold Corp's financial report for Q2 of 2025 shows a revenue of $23.0 million, with $50.9 million for the six months ending June 30, 2025. This marks a 21% increase from the same period in 2024. Net income was $1.6 million in Q2 and $4.8 million for the six months, compared to a $0.6 million loss in 2024. EBITDA for the quarter was $9.1 million and $21.5 million for the six months ending June 30, 2025. Soma sold 5,200 AuEq ounces in Q2 and 12,043 ounces in total for the first half of the year, a decrease from 2024. The average realized cash margin per gold ounce sold increased by 70% in comparison to 2024. Additionally, the company made debt payments of $3.5 million and converted $10.0 million of the loan to equity. Operations produced 5,377 AuEq ounces in Q2 and 12,020 ounces in total for the six months ending June 30, 2025. VANCOUVER, BC, Aug. 28, 2025 /CNW/ - Soma Gold Corp. has filed its Financial Statements and MD&A for Q2 of 2025 on SEDAR and the company's website.

data.title: Dow Jones Futures drop as Fed inflation data approaches; S&P 500 hits new high after Nvidia earnings; 7 stocks surge.
data.description: Following the release of Nvidia's earnings report, the S&P 500 reached a new peak, with Rubrik and Micron leading the surge in stock prices. As market optimism prevails, attention now turns to the looming release of inflation data by the Federal Reserve. In related news, Dow Jones Futures experienced a decline ahead of the inflation data announcement. Additionally, 7 new stocks are being recommended for purchase following Nvidia's successful performance in the market.
Hong Kong overnight HIBOR increases to 4% for initial period since May
